nodes.name: Manual Trigger
nodes.notes:
Starts the one-off migration manually.

Use this workflow for ad hoc imports, not for a recurring sync.
Before running, decide which config node you want active:
- **Set Config - View** for importing everything visible in a ClickUp view
- **Set Config - Task Root** for importing one root task tree

This trigger currently feeds the task-root config branch. To run in view mode, connect/activate the view config path instead.

nodes.name: Sticky Note
nodes.notes:
ClickUp View/List → Nextcloud Deck (one-off import)

This workflow supports two source modes:
1. **view** = resolve the real home list from a ClickUp view, then import parent tasks from that list
2. **task_root** = fetch one ClickUp root task, discover its home list, collect its descendants, and import only that subtree

Shared behavior:
- parent tasks become Deck cards
- subtasks are appended into the parent card description
- ClickUp comments are appended after subtasks/checklists
- Deck stacks are created from mapped ClickUp statuses
- Deck labels are created from OKR, Progress, Priority, and Finished
- completed ClickUp tasks are marked done in Deck after card creation

parameters.content:
ClickUp View/List → Nextcloud Deck (one-off import)

How this version works:
1. Uses the ClickUp view id from Set Config to resolve the real home list id.
2. Fetches the full list with subtasks=true and include_timl=true.
3. Imports only parent tasks as Deck cards and appends subtasks into the parent card description.
4. Appends task comments to the end of the card description.
5. Creates Deck labels from ClickUp OKR, Progress, and Priority, then assigns them to cards.
6. Sends due dates to Deck in ISO-8601 format.

Before you run:
- Keep the Set Config values as they are unless you want a different view/board.
- Attach the same Nextcloud HTTP Basic Auth credential to the Deck HTTP nodes.
- Best used as a one-off migration on a clean or lightly used board.

New in this version:
7. A second manual trigger can import one ClickUp root task tree into the same downstream Deck migration flow.

nodes.name: Deck - Validate Board
nodes.notes:
Validates the Nextcloud Deck target before any ClickUp-heavy work starts.

What it checks:
- base URL resolves
- Deck app API path is correct
- board ID exists
- attached HTTP Basic Auth credential can access the board

If this node fails, fix the Nextcloud URL, board ID, or credential attachment before debugging anything else.

nodes.name: Build Page Numbers
nodes.notes:
View-mode bootstrap.

Creates a single seed item:
- `page = 0`
- `clickup_list_id` from config

Why this exists:
- the view endpoint is queried first only to discover the actual home list ID behind the view
- full pagination starts later after the real list ID is known

parameters.jsCode:
const cfg = $('Set Config').first().json;
return [{ json: { page: 0, clickup_list_id: cfg.clickup_list_id } }];

nodes.name: ClickUp - Get View Tasks Page
nodes.notes:
Calls ClickUp's **view tasks** endpoint for page 0.

Request behavior:
- includes subtasks
- includes closed tasks

Purpose:
- gather enough task data to infer the real ClickUp home list ID used by the view
- this is not the full import yet; it is a discovery step

nodes.name: Append Comments To Task
nodes.notes:
Merges ClickUp comments into each prepared task description.

Formatting behavior:
- comments are normalized into markdown
- author and date are included when available
- comments are appended under a `Comments` section after the existing description

Output is the final card content used for card creation.

nodes.name: Build Stack Items
nodes.notes:
Collects the Deck stacks that should exist.

Logic:
- starts from `status_stack_map_json`
- also adds unmapped statuses observed in normalized tasks
- normalizes keys and sorts stacks by configured order

Result:
- one item per target Deck stack to create/read back later

nodes.name: Deck - Create Stack
nodes.notes:
Creates each target stack in Nextcloud Deck.

Implementation details:
- sends one request per stack
- `continueOnFail=true` so reruns or duplicate-stack situations do not stop the workflow
- the workflow later re-reads stacks from Deck, so exact create responses are not the final source of truth

nodes.name: After Stack Creation - Emit Once
nodes.notes:
Collapses the per-stack stream back to a single item.

Purpose:
- later phases only need to know that stack creation was attempted
- prevents label creation from running once per stack

nodes.name: Deck - Get Stacks
nodes.notes:
Reads the board's stacks after stack creation.

Why this matters:
- stack IDs are required to create cards in the correct stack
- this read is the authoritative stack source, which is safer than trusting create responses during reruns

nodes.name: Prepare Task Work Items
nodes.notes:
Combines normalized tasks with live Deck board data.

Main jobs:
- map each task to the correct `stack_id`
- map label titles to actual `label_id` values from the board
- create a stable card order within each stack
- carry forward title, description, due date, and label metadata for later steps

If expected stacks are missing, this node throws an error early.

nodes.name: Deck - Create Card
nodes.notes:
Creates one Deck card per prepared parent task.

Current mapping:
- title
- description
- order
- due date
- card type = `plain`

Notes:
- only parent tasks become cards
- subtasks remain embedded inside the description
- `continueOnFail=true` allows the workflow to continue into summary/reporting even if some card creates fail

parameters.body: ={{ JSON.stringify({ title: $json.title, type: 'plain', order: Number($json.order || 999), description: $json.description || '', duedate: $json.duedate || null }) }}

nodes.name: Result Summary
nodes.notes:
Produces a human-readable migration summary.

Reported metrics include:
- parent tasks attempted
- cards created and failed
- done-mark attempts and failures
- label assignment attempts and failures
- target board ID
- original ClickUp source ID

Use this node first when validating a run or troubleshooting partial imports.

nodes.name: Resolve ClickUp Home List ID
nodes.notes:
Extracts the real ClickUp list ID from the view results.

Output:
- `clickup_actual_list_id`
- `clickup_actual_list_name`
- `visible_task_count`
- original view ID for traceability

Fallback behavior:
- if tasks do not reveal a list ID, the config value is used only when it already looks like a numeric list ID
- otherwise the workflow throws an error

nodes.name: Build List Page Numbers
nodes.notes:
Creates one item per ClickUp list page for view mode.

Uses:
- `clickup_actual_list_id`
- `max_pages`

Output shape:
- page number
- actual list ID/name
- original view ID

This is the real pagination fan-out for the shared import pipeline.

nodes.name: ClickUp - Get List Tasks Page
nodes.notes:
Fetches paged tasks from the resolved ClickUp list.

Request options:
- `subtasks=true`
- `include_closed=true`
- `include_timl=true`

Purpose:
- pull the list contents that will be normalized into parent Deck cards
- multiple pages are requested based on `max_pages`

nodes.name: ClickUp - Get Task Comments
nodes.notes:
Fetches comments for each normalized parent task.

Important settings:
- batched one at a time
- 800 ms interval to be gentle with ClickUp
- `continueOnFail=true` so comment failures do not block card creation

Comments are appended into the Deck card description in the next node.

nodes.name: Build Label Items
nodes.notes:
Collects unique Deck labels from normalized tasks.

Labels currently come from:
- OKR custom field
- Progress custom field
- Priority
- Finished

It also normalizes colors to 6-digit hex values accepted by Deck.

nodes.name: Deck - Create Label
nodes.notes:
Creates each label in the target Deck board.

Implementation details:
- one request per label
- `continueOnFail=true` so reruns or duplicate labels do not halt the workflow
- actual label IDs are retrieved later by re-reading the board

nodes.name: After Label Creation - Emit Once
nodes.notes:
Collapses label-create results to one item.

Purpose:
- continue the workflow once after the full label batch
- avoid repeating downstream read/preparation work for each label

nodes.name: Deck - Get Board After Labels
nodes.notes:
Reads the board again after label creation.

Why this matters:
- Deck label IDs are needed for assignment later
- the board response is treated as the authoritative label list after any create/duplicate behavior

nodes.name: Prepare Label Assignments
nodes.notes:
Builds one output item per `(card, label)` assignment.

Input assumptions:
- the card was successfully created
- label IDs were already resolved from the board

This fan-out happens after card creation because Deck labels are attached to existing cards, not sent inline during create.

nodes.name: Deck - Assign Label
nodes.notes:
Assigns one label at a time to a created Deck card.

Implementation details:
- uses board ID, stack ID, card ID, and label ID
- `continueOnFail=true` so a single label problem does not stop the summary
- success/failure counts are reported at the end

nodes.name: Prepare Completed Card Updates
nodes.notes:
Builds updates for cards that should be marked done.

Only tasks recognized as complete in ClickUp are included.
The payload carries:
- board/stack/card IDs
- title/description/order
- due date
- `done` timestamp
- owner fallback information

Community note:
- this logic references `nextcloud_username` as a fallback owner, but that field is not defined in the current config nodes. It usually still works if Deck returns an owner on card creation; if not, add that config field before sharing widely.

nodes.name: Deck - Mark Card Done
nodes.notes:
Updates already-created Deck cards to mark them done.

Why this is a separate step:
- cards are first created normally
- completed ClickUp tasks are then patched with a `done` timestamp

The request also re-sends title/description/order (and due date when present) to keep the update payload complete.

nodes.name: Set Config - View
nodes.notes:
Configuration for **view mode**.

Fill these placeholders before running:
- `clickup_list_id` = **ClickUp view ID** in this mode (the field name is legacy)
- `clickup_api_token` = ClickUp personal/team API token
- `nextcloud_base_url` = your Nextcloud base URL without extra path
- `nextcloud_deck_board_id` = target Deck board ID
- `max_pages` = maximum number of ClickUp list pages to read
- `status_stack_map_json` = mapping from ClickUp status to Deck stack title/order
- `subtask_title_prefix` = prefix shown before subtask names in the card description

Important:
- `source_mode` must stay `view`
- this node contains placeholders, not real secrets
- downstream nodes expect `status_stack_map_json` to be valid JSON

nodes.name: Normalize ClickUp Parent Tasks - View
nodes.notes:
Transforms the full list payload from view mode into the workflow's shared task schema.

Main jobs:
- deduplicate tasks across pages
- keep only parent tasks as card candidates
- map ClickUp statuses to Deck stacks
- build markdown description with metadata, description, checklists, and subtasks
- derive Deck labels from OKR, Progress, Priority, and completion state
- convert due/completion dates to ISO format

Output fields are designed for downstream stack/label/card creation.

nodes.name: Set Config - Task Root
nodes.notes:
Configuration for **task_root mode**.

Fill these placeholders before running:
- `clickup_task_id` = the ClickUp root task ID whose full subtree should be imported
- `clickup_api_token` = ClickUp personal/team API token
- `nextcloud_base_url` = your Nextcloud base URL
- `nextcloud_deck_board_id` = target Deck board ID
- `max_pages` = how many pages of the home list to scan while finding descendants
- `status_stack_map_json` = status-to-stack mapping
- `subtask_title_prefix` = prefix added before subtask names

Important:
- `source_mode` must stay `task_root`
- this path is useful when one list is huge but you only want one project/task tree
- this node contains placeholders, not real secrets

nodes.name: Set Config
nodes.notes:
Pass-through normalizer for the chosen config node.

Purpose:
- whichever config node runs upstream, this code node copies its JSON forward unchanged
- downstream nodes always read settings from **Set Config**, so both source modes can share the same later logic

nodes.name: IF - Task Root Mode
nodes.notes:
Routes execution by `source_mode`.

Branches:
- **false / upper branch**: view mode → use ClickUp view to discover the real list, then import all parent tasks from that list
- **true / lower branch**: task-root mode → fetch the chosen root task, discover its home list, then keep only that task subtree

This node is the switch that allows one shared downstream migration pipeline.

nodes.name: ClickUp - Get Root Task
nodes.notes:
Fetches the specific ClickUp task referenced by `clickup_task_id`.

Purpose:
- verify the task exists
- get the task name/URL
- discover the task's home list so the workflow can scan that list for descendants

nodes.name: Resolve Root Task Home List ID
nodes.notes:
Extracts root-task metadata needed by the task-root branch.

Output:
- root task ID/name/URL
- the home list ID/name

The workflow stops here if the root task or its home list cannot be resolved.

nodes.name: Build Root Task List Page Numbers
nodes.notes:
Creates page items for scanning the root task's home list.

Uses:
- discovered home list ID
- configured `max_pages`

Why scan the full list:
- ClickUp's task API returns the root task, but descendants may need to be found from paged list results before the subtree can be isolated

nodes.name: ClickUp - Get Root Task List Tasks Page
nodes.notes:
Fetches pages from the root task's home list.

Request options mirror the main list import:
- subtasks included
- closed tasks included
- `include_timl=true`

These pages are later filtered down to only the chosen root task and its descendants.

nodes.name: Filter Root Task Tree
nodes.notes:
Builds the root-task subtree.

Logic:
- starts with the fetched root task
- adds paged list tasks into a map
- walks parent relationships until all descendants of the root task are included

Output:
- one payload containing `tasks` limited to the selected subtree

nodes.name: Normalize ClickUp Task Tree
nodes.notes:
Normalizes the task-root subtree into the same schema produced by the view branch.

This keeps downstream logic shared:
- same task/card structure
- same stack mapping
- same description building
- same label derivation
- same completion and due-date formatting

nodes.name: Normalize ClickUp Parent Tasks
nodes.notes:
Merge point for both branches.

This node intentionally does no transformation and simply forwards all incoming items.
Its value is structural:
- view-mode normalized tasks and task-root normalized tasks can join here
- downstream nodes only need one connection path

parameters.content:
Setup checklist for community use

1. Replace all `INSERT-...-HERE` placeholders in the active config node.
2. Reconnect the Nextcloud HTTP Basic Auth credential on every Deck HTTP node after import.
3. In **view** mode, `clickup_list_id` actually expects a ClickUp **view ID**.
4. In **task_root** mode, `clickup_task_id` is the root task whose subtree will be imported.
5. `status_stack_map_json` must stay valid JSON.

Security audit of this export
- I did **not** find any real ClickUp API token, username, or password in config values.
- The workflow contains credential **references** (`id` / `name`) for n8n credentials, not the secret values themselves.
- The file also contains n8n metadata such as workflow IDs and instance ID; those are metadata, not passwords.
- One credential display name includes your first name (`Nextcloud app password`). That is not the password, but you may still want to rename it before publishing the workflow screenshot/export publicly.

Upper branch = View mode

Flow:
Set Config - View → Set Config → Validate Deck board → IF false branch
→ seed page 0 on ClickUp view endpoint
→ resolve the real home list ID
→ paginate the list
→ normalize parent tasks into the shared schema

Why this extra discovery step exists:
ClickUp views are not the same thing as ClickUp lists. The workflow first uses the view to discover the underlying list, then imports from the list endpoint.

Lower branch = Task root mode

Flow:
Set Config - Task Root → Set Config → Validate Deck board → IF true branch
→ fetch one root task
→ resolve its home list
→ paginate that list
→ filter down to the root task and all descendants
→ normalize into the same shared schema as view mode

Use this when you want one project/task tree instead of everything in a ClickUp view or list.

Shared pipeline after both branches merge

1. Build unique Deck stacks from mapped statuses
2. Create stacks
3. Build unique Deck labels from normalized tasks
4. Create labels
5. Re-read board and stacks to get authoritative IDs
6. Prepare work items with stack IDs, label IDs, order, due date, and description
7. Fetch ClickUp comments and append them to the card description

Important design choice:
The workflow trusts the **read-back** data from Deck more than the create responses. That makes reruns safer when stacks or labels already exist.

Final phase

- Create one Deck card per parent task
- Fan out label assignments and apply them
- For tasks already complete in ClickUp, patch the created card with a `done` timestamp
- Emit a result summary with counts and failures

Behavior worth knowing
- Subtasks are not separate cards; they are embedded in the parent card description.
- Comment fetches, card creation, label assignment, and done updates use `continueOnFail` where appropriate so one bad item does not sink the entire run.

main.node: Resolve Root Task Home List ID
Build Root Task List Page Numbers
ClickUp - Get Root Task List Tasks Page
